content.problemStatement:
Chef is learning linear algebra. Recently, he learnt that for a square matrix MMM, trace(M)\mathop{\rm trace}(M)trace(M) is defined as the sum of all elements on the main diagonal of MMM (an element lies on the main diagonal if its row index and column index are equal).

Now, Chef wants to solve some excercises related to this new quantity, so he wrote down a square matrix AAA with size N√óNN\times NN√óN. A square submatrix of AAA with size l√óll\times ll√ól is a contiguous block of l√óll\times ll√ól elements of AAA. Formally, if BBB is a submatrix of AAA with size l√óll\times ll√ól, then there must be integers rrr and ccc (1‚â§r,c‚â§N+1‚àíl1\le r, c \le N+1-l1‚â§r,c‚â§N+1‚àíl) such that Bi,j=Ar+i‚àí1,c+j‚àí1B_{i,j} = A_{r+i-1, c+j-1}Bi,j‚Äã=Ar+i‚àí1,c+j‚àí1‚Äã for each 1‚â§i,j‚â§l1 \le i, j \le l1‚â§i,j‚â§l.

Help Chef find the maximum trace of a square submatrix of AAA.
constraints: 1‚â§Ai,j‚â§1001 \le A_{i,j} \le 1001‚â§Ai,j‚Äã‚â§100 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains NNN space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,NA_{i,1}, A_{i,2}, \dots, A_{i, N}Ai,1‚Äã,Ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai,N‚Äã denoting the iii-th row of the matrix AAA.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum possible trace.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The submatrix with the largest trace is
6 3
2 7

which has trace equal to 6+7=136 + 7 = 136+7=13. (This submatrix is obtained for r=2,c=1,l=2r=2, c=1, l=2r=2,c=1,l=2.)
